review.planStatus: ALL_TASKS_DONE (task 9.3 actually DONE via commit aa0362; plan log stale — see M2)

findings.title: yaml not added to the allowed-dependency table
findings.description: The spec (NFR-7) and plan (Phase 1.1) assert 'yaml and ajv are on the allowed-dependency list in typescript.md'. The table includes ajv (L424) but NOT yaml. typescript.md was edited in this change (union illustration), so the omission is a missed edit. Non-blocking: DEC-1 is the binding approval and the license/transitive audit passes (yaml@2.9.0 ISC, 0 transitive deps).
findings.suggestedFix: Add row: | yaml | YAML parsing (marksync.yml + front-matter, DEC-1) | Latest |

findings.title: Plan log records doc reconciliation (task 9.3) as skipped/deferred but commit aa036c2 performed it
findings.description: The execution log + Phase 9 acceptance say 9.3 was 'intentionally skipped' / 'DEFERRED to Phase 7', but commit aa036c2 reconciled all four docs. The work is done; the bookkeeping is stale and self-contradictory. Misleads dod_check (Phase 10) and the PR reviewer.
findings.suggestedFix: Update task 9.3 checkbox to [x], the Execution Log row 9 note, and the Phase 9 'system docs reconciled' acceptance to PASSED (ref commit aa036c2).

findings.title: Redundant Result type-only alias import
findings.description: Imports Result as a value (L26) AND Result as ResultType (type-only, L27). Under verbatimModuleSyntax the single value import is usable in both positions, so ResultType is redundant.
findings.suggestedFix: Drop L27 and use Result<void, ConfigError> at the return-type site, or drop the value import if only the type is needed.

findings.title: A few defensive/invariant branches are uncovered by tests
findings.description: Coverage 100% funcs / 91.81% lines. Uncovered lines are defensive paths (write-failure catch L119-125, non-ENOENT read branch config.ts:74-76, assertNeverMarkSyncError never-arms errors.ts:106-112, some keyword branches config-errors.ts:50-54/92-98). Acceptable per testing strategy (invariant coverage > raw line coverage).
findings.suggestedFix: Optional: a fault-injection test for the write-failure path. No change required.
